name: Science City, Kolkata
concept&appearance: Developed on a 50-acre site that was formerly a municipal garbage dump for over 100 years, representing an ecological reclamation project. The complex features a unique architectural blend of geometric forms: the Dynamotion Hall is designed as a massive spiral ziggurat rising to 38 meters, while the Space Odyssey building features a 23-meter diameter tilted dome. The Convention Centre resembles a tilted saucer with a truncated spherical cone. The extensive landscaping and Science Park integrate the buildings with the environment, dispelling the site's history as a dumping ground.
permanent_exhibitions: 1. Dynamotion Hall: A spiral building housing the 'Hall of Illusions' (visual perception exhibits), 'Powers of Ten' (illustrating the universe's scale), a Fresh Water Aquarium with 26 tanks, and a Live Butterfly Enclave. It also features the 'Science on a Sphere' projection system. 2. Space Odyssey: Houses the Space Theatre (planetarium), 3D Vision Theatre, Time Machine (motion simulator), and 'Mirror Magic' exhibits, focusing on space science and reflection. 3. Earth Exploration Hall: A two-storied hemispherical building displaying the details of the Southern Hemisphere (ground floor) and Northern Hemisphere (first floor), centered around a huge sliced globe model explaining geology, geography, flora, and fauna. 4. Maritime Centre: A ship-shaped building showcasing India's maritime history, navigation systems, and artifacts in collaboration with Kolkata Port Trust. 5. Science Exploration Hall: A newer facility (opened 2016) featuring the 'Evolution of Life' (dark ride), 'Panorama on Human Evolution' (360-degree projection), 'Emerging Technologies' gallery, and 'Science and Technology Heritage of India'. 6. Science Park: An outdoor open-air museum with exhibits like the Caterpillar Ride, Gravity Coaster, and musical fountain, allowing visitors to interact with physics principles in a natural setting.
central_hall: The Dynamotion Hall features a large double-height central atrium that rises to the top of the structure, illuminated by a glazed skylight. This central space serves as a major circulation node and includes iconic installations like the 'Rolling Balls' exhibit that descends through the spiral ramp, demonstrating conversion of potential to kinetic energy.
special_effects_theaters: 1. Space Theatre: A 23-meter diameter tilted dome equipped with a Helios Star Ball planetarium and high-resolution full-dome digital projection system (formerly Astrovision 10/70), seating 360 people. 2. 3D Vision Theatre: Uses Polaroid-based stereo back projection for 3D immersive films. 3. Time Machine: A 30-seater motion simulator capsule providing virtual journeys with six degrees of motion. 4. Evolution of Life - Dark Ride: An automated ride-through exhibit with animatronics and audio-visual effects. 5. Panorama on Human Evolution: A large cylindrical hall with a 360-degree static panoramic projection depicting human evolution.
science_popularization_activities: The museum organizes regular 'Science Shows' (live demonstrations of scientific principles), 'Science on a Sphere' educational sessions, and vacation creative science camps for students. It also hosts commemorative programs for events like National Science Day and World Environment Day, along with teacher training programs and interactive workshops aligned with school curriculums.
